label: Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees

node.label: Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees
node.definition: Decision trees recursively partition feature space; random forests average decorrelated trees, and gradient boosting adds trees that correct earlier residual error.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees is modeled as depending on Recursive partitioning & ensembling, Supervised learning and feeding downstream nodes such as Recommendation, ranking & personalization, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: atlas downstream link to Recommendation, ranking & personalization
atlas downstream link to Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Recursive partitioning & ensembling, Supervised learning.

dependencies.rationale: Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recursive partitioning & ensembling.
Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.

In this atlas graph, Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) because Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
